scanner_mode_reason: scan.sh execution blocked by sandbox approval gate; ran scan.sh's HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW pattern library inline via Grep per SKILL.md sandbox fallback
note: First run — all findings are baseline; delta computation begins next run. The 769 HIGH matches against the pattern `\`[^\`]*\$` come almost entirely from markdown inline code referencing template paths like `\`memory/logs/${today}.md\`` and are not shell command substitution. They are NOT individually persisted here — only the structured per-file pattern-count summary is, so the next scan can detect a meaningful delta without a 1.5MB state file.

findings_summary_by_file.note: Pattern `\`[^\`]*\$` matches escaped backticks in echo strings writing markdown into a results file. Not command substitution.
Legitimate Vercel API calls with env-var query params. Auth via header, not URL.
Legitimate API POST with env-var URL prefix.

Legitimate Replicate API calls with bearer token in header.
Scanner's own pattern definitions match themselves. Self-reference.

Line 578: `${{ github.event.action }}` interpolated directly into `run:` block — same anti-pattern as 2026-04-11 fix. Lower risk because `action` values are constrained by dispatcher, but still bind via `env:` indirection. Other matches are legitimate curl-with-token API calls. Line 753 `git push --force-with-lease` matches `git push --force` MEDIUM pattern (acceptable usage).

Lines 86, 94, 96, 718 interpolate `${{ inputs.skill }}` / `${{ steps.skill.outputs.name }}` / `${{ steps.work.outputs.label }}` into `run:` blocks. inputs.skill comes from workflow_dispatch (requires write auth) so low real exposure, but anti-pattern matches the 2026-04-11 incident class. Other matches are legitimate curl-with-token API calls. Line 725 `git push --force-with-lease` matches MEDIUM pattern (acceptable usage).
curl-with-env-var matches inside fenced code blocks documenting API usage — code-fence downgrade applied. Seed-suppressed in baseline.
